meta.homeTitle: De Huur Radar | Free rental scam check & risk score 0–100
meta.homeDescription: Seen a flat or a room for rent? Review the listing before you pay: risk score 0–100 with explanations, city rent benchmark, and clear signals (WhatsApp, upfront payment, wire transfer). For every renter — from studios to family homes — and just as useful for students and rooms. Marktplaats, Funda, Facebook. No account.

brand.name: De Huur Radar
brand.tagline: Seen a rental home or a room? Review the listing before you pay — clear rules, no secret score.

a11y.footerStatement: Text-based, no video or audio. Zoom, keyboard, and screen reader use are supported.

trust.pillar2: Fixed fraud signals, in plain sight
trust.line: The score uses fixed, checkable criteria in the text: e.g. price vs. benchmark, risky payment routes, WhatsApp/Telegram, urgency, no viewing, ID requests, external forms — each with set points. The full list is on the analysis page. Optional AI; no hidden weighting.

hero.title: Seen a rental home or a room? Review the listing before you pay
hero.subtitle: Risk score 0–100 with clear reasons — not a black box. For flats, studios, and rooms; students and room seekers are welcome here too. Start below or open the analysis.

home.cardCheckBody: Paste text or a URL, optionally with OCR from a screenshot. You get score, signals, and a report — no sign-up.

check.metaTitle: De Huur Radar — Analyze | Rental home or room, risk score, benchmark & fraud signals
check.metaDescription: Paste a listing URL or text (rental home or room). Risk score 0–100 with reasons: price vs. benchmark, WhatsApp, upfront payment, wire transfer. OCR on screenshots. Free, no account.
check.pageLead: Paste text or a link — for a room, studio, or whole flat. You get a risk score with clear reasons, including when you’re hunting for a student room.

about.metaTitle: About De Huur Radar — why, what, and how
about.metaDescription: Why review a listing before you pay, what you get (score, benchmark, signals, report), and how De Huur Radar works — transparent rules, optional AI, no account.
about.title: Why, what, and how
about.missionBody: De Huur Radar helps you assess a rental home or room listing before you send money or sensitive details. We use fixed, checkable rules — no hidden scoring — and optionally a language model for extra context. No account: paste text or a link and get a risk score with clear reasons.
about.whyLead: Rental fraud is common on classifieds, Facebook, room sites, and spam. Scammers exploit scarcity, trust, and urgency.
about.whyPoint1: Prices that look too good and a convincing story attract quick replies; then comes pressure for deposits or upfront payment.
about.whyPoint2: A second look before you pay saves stress, time, and money — even if you have rented before.
about.whyPoint3: This is a tool, not a guarantee: you stay responsible, but you get a structured list of red flags and a benchmark sanity check.
about.whatPoint1: A risk score from 0–100 with an explanation of which signals count (full rules on the analysis page, expandable under “What we do”).
about.whatPoint2: A rough comparison to model rent for a city or region (€/month), and sometimes €/m² when price and floor area are in the text — indicative, not a valuation.
about.whatPoint3: Concrete signals: payment route, urgency, contact (WhatsApp, etc.), forms; plus an estimate of how much the text looks like a rental listing (this does not change the score). Optional AI observations if you enable that mode.
about.whatPoint4: A report (web and/or PDF) to keep or share — no sign-up required.
about.howShortStep1: Paste listing text, a public URL, or use OCR on a screenshot — processed locally in your browser until you click Analyze.
about.howShortStep2: Our server reads price, location, text patterns and sometimes m², estimates dwelling type and rental sector, applies fixed rules and a benchmark, and computes a score.
about.howShortStep3: You see the score, signals, recommendations, and (optional) AI narrative; technical details and every point weight are on the analysis page.
about.howShortHint: Want every step, rule points, and benchmark detail? On the analysis page, open the collapsible section “What we do — and how the score is built”. Or see the FAQ for common questions.

howItWorks.title: What we do — and how the score is built
howItWorks.lead: We give a risk score from 0 to 100 using fixed, checkable rules. There is no hidden weighting: the steps and points below are what the server actually uses.
howItWorks.summaryToggle: Full explanation: steps, points, and benchmark
howItWorks.step1: Input: pasted text, a URL (public page), or text from a screenshot (Marktplaats/Facebook, etc.) via OCR in your browser. Works for flats, houses, and student rooms; we read price, contact, and description. We also estimate dwelling type (room vs whole home/studio) and rental sector, sometimes extract floor area (m²), and show separately how much the content looks like a rental listing (that last estimate does not change the score).
howItWorks.step2: City and benchmark: we try to detect a municipality (from text and URL, e.g. “street, city”) and use a fixed table of model rent (€/month) per municipality, including aliases when an older place name appears in the ad. Unknown locations use a national reference. This is a rough estimate, not a valuation.
howItWorks.step3: Rule score: only what we find in the text counts. Points from all matches are added together, capped at 100.
howItWorks.step4: Optional AI: turn the switch on for an extra pass with observations and narrative alongside the fixed rules. The final score is the maximum of the rule score and the AI score; with the switch off, only the rule engine runs.
howItWorks.step5: Recommendations and checklists come from the rules (sometimes with extra tips for rooms or social housing). With AI on, you also get further suggestions and observations.
howItWorks.rulePrice: Price well below benchmark (+30): monthly rent under 70% of the model average for the detected city.
howItWorks.ruleMessaging: Chat contact (+10 WhatsApp, +8 Telegram/Signal/WeChat/Skype): many scams avoid traceable email or landlines.
howItWorks.ruleUrgency: Time pressure (+20): e.g. “today only”, “respond quickly”, “limited time”, “within 24 hours”.
howItWorks.rulePayment: High-risk payment (+40): Western Union, MoneyGram, pay in advance, crypto (BTC/USDT), gift cards, Paysafecard.
howItWorks.ruleTrust: Viewing & distance (+12): no normal viewing, keys by post/courier, or “I live abroad”.
howItWorks.ruleIdentity: ID / privacy (+10 each): request for ID before a viewing, or an “exchange ID” pattern with a stranger — each pattern counts separately.
howItWorks.ruleFees: Money before viewing (+14): registration or “admin” fees before an appointment.
howItWorks.ruleForms: External form (+10): Google Forms, Typeform, etc. — common in mass copy-paste scams.
howItWorks.ruleNarrative: Classic scam story (+10): moving abroad, inheritance, “charity” — often copy-paste.
howItWorks.ruleTemplateEn: English template (+8): phrases like “Kind regards”, “Dear Sir” — typical of cross-border fraud.
howItWorks.benchmarkBody: First we try to infer which municipality applies; then we compare to a fixed model amount (€/month) per municipality from our internal table. Unknown locations use a national reference. When price and usable floor area (m²) are in the text, we also show indicative rent per m² (including for small rooms/studios) — still indicative, not a valuation.
howItWorks.disclaimer: This is a support tool, not legal or financial advice and not a guarantee against fraud. Always use your own judgment.

form.label: Listing text or URL
form.placeholder: Paste the full ad, or only a link such as 123wonen.nl/…
form.hint: Or use a screenshot below (Marktplaats, Facebook, etc.): text is read locally in your browser. For URLs: public listing pages over HTTP, no login.
form.useAiHint: Off: fixed rules only (faster). On: extra narrative alongside the score — only if our server is set up for it.
form.progressTitle: Hang on, we’re working on it:

ocr.title: Text from a screenshot
ocr.hint: Useful for Marktplaats, Facebook Marketplace, or other apps: take a screen capture and extract text into the field.
ocr.privacy: OCR runs in your browser; only when you click Analyze is the text sent to our server.
ocr.errorEmpty: No text found. Try a sharper screenshot or paste manually.
ocr.errorGeneric: Text recognition failed. Try again or paste manually.

result.marketDiff: Your price differs by {pct}% from that benchmark.
result.marketNoPrice: No price in the text to compare.
result.listingFitAria: Listing detection: match score {n} out of 100
result.listingFitTitle: Does this look like a rental listing?
result.listingFitStrong: Yes — it looks like a listing
result.listingFitStrongBody: The text (and URL, if any) has typical signals such as rent, housing terms, size, or a known rental platform. Not a guarantee, but a clear hint this is meant as rental housing information.
result.listingFitMixed: Unclear — could be a listing or not
result.listingFitMixedBody: There are some signals, but the text is short, lacks clear rental details, or mixes in other page content. If unsure, paste the full ad or a direct listing link.
result.listingFitWeakBody: Little here looks like a rental ad: few recognizable terms, very short text, or a page without typical rental info. The fraud check is less reliable — try the full listing text or a real listing URL.
result.benchmarkAria: Explanation: which region and model amount we use
result.benchmarkIntroMunicipality: We map this listing to {city} when possible. For that area we use an indicative model rent of {avg} per month — not a valuation, but a quick check: does this price look normal for that region?
result.benchmarkIntroNational: We could not detect a specific municipality in the text. We then use a national model of {avg} per month as a reference (same order of magnitude as a broad market average; not a valuation).
result.benchmarkVerdictNoPrice: Without a visible monthly rent in the ad we cannot compare to the model — add the price to the text if needed.
result.benchmarkVerdictFarBelow: Your price is {pct}% below that model — that stands out as “much cheaper than normal for this area” and weighs heavily in the risk score (even if other explanations exist).
result.benchmarkVerdictBelow: Your price is {pct}% below the model — it can still be suspicious; double-check the listing.
result.benchmarkVerdictAround: Your price is about {pct}% from the model — a reasonable band around what we expect for this area (still: use your own judgment).
result.benchmarkVerdictAbove: Your price is {pct}% above the model — that is not a fraud signal by itself; we mainly flag “too good to be true” low prices.
result.listingFactsPerM2ModelNote: derived from model monthly rent ÷ {m2} m²
result.listingFactsSmallSurfaceNote: €/m² is often higher than for larger homes; this is a rough comparison, not a valuation.
result.listingFactsScopeNational: Benchmark: national average — no specific city detected in the text.
result.listingFactsScopeCity: Benchmark: model for {city} (indicative, not a valuation).
result.dwellingConfMedium: limited signals — read with extra care
result.dwellingConfLow: little to go on in the text

errors.generic: Analysis failed. Try again in a moment or check your connection.

faq.metaTitle: Rental fraud FAQ & safe renting | De Huur Radar
faq.metaDescription: Frequently asked questions about rental fraud, student rooms, WhatsApp scams, and trusted sources. Links to consumer and government advice. Contact and further reading.
faq.contactIntro: Questions about this tool or the website? Email:
faq.contactNote: For legal or financial questions about a specific tenancy, consult a professional or official channels (see the sources above).
faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: De Huur Radar combines fixed text rules with an optional AI layer. You can use it for whole homes, flats, and student rooms — anywhere you can paste a rental listing. Below are common questions. At the bottom you will find external sources for further reading — not an endorsement.
faq.sourcesIntro: This selection (40+ links) reflects common guidance on rental fraud in the Netherlands and the EU: government, regulators, tenant organisations, platforms, and EU resources — including for students and room seekers. When in doubt, verify ownership via the land registry (Kadaster) and be sceptical of “too good to be true” offers, chat-only contact, and paying upfront.
faq.q1: What does De Huur Radar do?
faq.a1: You paste listing text or a URL. We estimate a risk score (0–100) using detectable patterns (price vs. a model benchmark, pressure to pay quickly, risky payment routes, chat-app contact, etc.). We also show dwelling type, rental sector, sometimes m² and €/m², and whether the text looks like a listing — the latter does not change the score. An optional language model may add a second score; the final score is the maximum of the rule score and the AI score.
faq.q2: Why is a low rent a warning sign?
faq.a2: Underpricing is a common lure. We roughly compare the extracted price to a city/region benchmark. This is not a valuation—just a quick sanity check, similar to consumer advice about “too good to be true”.
faq.q3: Should I reply via WhatsApp if the ad asks for it?
faq.a3: Not necessarily—and be extra careful if WhatsApp or Telegram is the only channel. Consumer organisations recommend verifying identity and tenancy rights and not relying on chat alone.
faq.q4: How can I check someone is allowed to let the property?
faq.a4: Ask for a tenancy agreement and verify who owns the property, e.g. via Kadaster ownership information (small fee). In sublets the main tenant/owner often must agree—see public legal information (e.g. Juridisch Loket, Rijksoverheid).
faq.q5: What are typical Facebook or classifieds scams?
faq.a5: Examples: stolen photos (use reverse image search), new profiles, “viewing reservation” fees, or pressure to transfer a deposit the same day. Articles from Huurstunt and platform safety pages describe these patterns in detail.
faq.a6: Collect evidence (screenshots, payment proof), stop contact, report to the police, notify Fraudehelpdesk.nl, and ask your bank about chargeback. Similar steps are published by Stekkies and national fraud helpdesks.
faq.a7: No. De Huur Radar is a technical tool with transparent rules; it does not replace an agent, lawyer, or official reporting channel.
faq.q8: Why do English scam phrases affect the score?
faq.a8: Cross-border scammers often use boilerplate English. If those phrases appear in a supposedly Dutch listing, we add a small risk signal—in addition to other checks.
faq.q9: Are student rooms different from regular rentals?
faq.a9: The scams are often the same: unrealistic rent, registration or “agency” fees before a viewing, pressure to pay fast, or swapping ID copies with a stranger. Room listings are a common target when demand is high. See e.g. Scholieren.com (Dutch, CCV / ministry tips: reverse image search, no brokerage fees as a tenant without a broker mandate, government Kopie ID app). De Huur Radar applies the same text patterns to room ads as to other rentals.
faq.q10: The site won’t load or I see an error (especially on mobile)—what should I do?
faq.a10: Often this is a stale browser cache or an old “app-like” install of the site. First try refreshing the page or opening the site in a private/incognito window. If it persists, clear data for this site only. Firefox (Android): menu → Settings → Delete browsing data → Cookies & site data / Site permissions → find dehuurradar.nl → remove. Chrome (Android): ⋮ → Settings → Privacy and security → Delete browsing data → include “Site settings” or use a private tab, then open the site again. You don’t need an account; this only clears temporary files on your phone.

footer.staleCacheHint: Site not loading? Refresh or clear site data for dehuurradar.nl — see FAQ question 10.
footer.note: De Huur Radar gives a risk indication using fixed rules, with an optional short AI summary. It is not a substitute for your own checks or legal advice.
footer.buildIdTitle: Timestamp of the last frontend build on this server (compare with local)

cookies.dialogIntro: This site does not use cookies for marketing, ad profiles, or visitor analytics. Below we explain what may be stored in your browser and why.
cookies.noTrackingBanner: No advertising or analytics tracking (no Meta Pixel, Google Analytics, etc.). No sale of personal data to third parties via this website.
cookies.necessaryOnlyHelp: Necessary only: we only store your choice for this banner and remove saved language and theme preferences. The page will reload.
cookies.apiNote: When you run an analysis, your browser sends an HTTPS request to our server with the text you pasted. Server logs may contain technical data (such as IP address and time) for security and debugging—not for ad tracking via browser cookies.
cookies.fontsNote: Fonts are loaded from Google Fonts (fonts.googleapis.com / fonts.gstatic.com). Your IP address may be processed by Google. We do not place marketing cookies for this.
cookies.legalNote: This overview is for transparency only. It is not legal advice; consult a privacy professional if in doubt.
cookies.footerSummary: No marketing or analytics cookies. Functional storage (language, theme) only after you choose. Fonts and storage details: cookie settings.

rows.name: wsc_privacy_consent

rows.duration: Until you clear data or change your choice

rows.name: wsc_locale_pref
rows.purpose: Language preference (EN/NL), only if you accept functional storage.
rows.duration: Until you clear data or change your choice

rows.name: wsc-color-mode
rows.duration: Until you clear data or change your choice
